scheme_name: Capital Subsidy to Existing Hotels for Renovation/Modernization & Upgradation
page_title: Capital Subsidy to Existing Hotels for Renovation/Modernization & Upgradation

details: The scheme "Capital Subsidy to Existing Hotels for Renovation/Modernization & Upgradation" was launched by the Department of Tourism, Government of Madhya Pradesh. The scheme aims to encourage the renovation, modernization, and upgradation of existing hotels and resorts into Deluxe or higher category hotels through private investment. The scheme provides a capital subsidy calculated as per the tourism policy guidelines for the respective category, subject to specific investment and capacity milestones. The applicant must be an existing hotel or resort unit making a minimum capital investment of ₹10,00,00,000/- or ₹25,00,00,000/- depending on the upgradation category. The scheme is implemented by the Madhya Pradesh Tourism Board. The applications for this scheme are accepted online through the Madhya Pradesh Government's Invest Portal.
benefits: Subsidy For Upgradation To Deluxe Hotels
Existing standard hotels (minimum 50 rooms) and mini resorts (minimum 20 rooms) upgrading to Deluxe Hotels/Resorts are eligible for capital subsidy as per the tourism policy under such category.
Subsidy For Upgradation To 4-Star or Higher

Existing Deluxe hotels and resorts upgrading to 4-star or higher category Hotels (minimum 75 rooms) or Resorts (minimum 25 rooms) are eligible for capital subsidy as per the tourism policy under such category.
An additional 5% Capital Investment Subsidy shall be given to tourism projects established in distant and ulterior areas.
It will be mandatory for all units, who have availed subsidy to run the units at least for three years from the date of availing the subsidy.
Such units will submit a self-declaration on or before 15th of April every year regarding continuous operation of the unit.
On non-compliance regarding the closure of the unit, the investor shall have to return the subsidy amount as follows:
Closure of unit within 1 year from the date of receipt of subsidy: 80% amount of subsidy has to be refunded.
Closure of unit within 2 years from the date of receipt of subsidy: 60% amount of subsidy to be refunded.
Closure of unit within 3 years from the date of receipt of subsidy: 50% amount of subsidy to be refunded.
eligibility: The applicant must be an existing Standard Hotel or Mini Resort to apply for Category A upgradation.
The applicant must ensure the upgraded facility meets the criteria for Deluxe Hotels (minimum 50 rooms) or Resorts (minimum 20 rooms) under Category A.
The applicant must make a minimum investment of ₹10,00,00,000/- to be eligible for the subsidy under Category A.
The applicant must be an existing Deluxe Hotel or Resort to apply for Category B upgradation.
The applicant must ensure the upgraded facility meets the criteria for 4-star or higher category Hotels (minimum 75 rooms) or Resorts (minimum 25 rooms) under Category B.
The applicant must make a minimum investment of ₹25,00,00,000/- to be eligible for the subsidy under Category B.
The applicant must claim subsidy only on capital investment made after the enforcement of this Policy.
The applicant must submit the application in the prescribed format within 1 year from the date of commencement of operation.
The applicant must not claim Investment Promotion Assistance if they are claiming capital subsidy under this policy.

offline: The applicant must visit the Madhya Pradesh Government's
and initiate the registration process as a "Vendor / Investor".

The applicant must fill in the basic information section by selecting the appropriate "Type of Organisation" from the available options, which include Public Limited Company, Private Limited, LLP, Proprietorship Firm, Partnership Firm, Cooperative, Trust, FPO, and Individual. The applicant must enter the "Firm/Company Name" and the "Authorized Person Name" to establish the identity of the entity.
The applicant must enter a valid email ID and mobile number. The Application Number, User ID, password, and all other important communications will be sent to this registered email ID.
The applicant must complete the basic registration form submission. The system generates a User ID and password, which are shared directly with the applicant's registered mobile number.

The authorized person logs in to the portal using the User ID and password received on the registered mobile number to process the company's activities. After the initial login, the applicant must update their profile to complete the full registration process before proceeding to apply for the scheme.
The applicant must apply for and secure mandatory permissions through the online single-window system within the stipulated time frames. Then, the applicant must do the following -
Complete the construction, ensuring the minimum capital expenditure is met.
Commence commercial operations and gather proof of operation.

The applicant must submit the capital subsidy application online within one year from the date of commencement of operation. Upload all required documents, ensuring all files meet the specified format requirements (e.g., JPG for photos).
The Tourism Department/Board verifies the application and documents and processes the subsidy claim.

documents_required: Company/Firm Documentation
Brief Note on the Business Antecedents of the Promoters (Not More Than 60 Words)
Copy of Proof of Legal Authorized Person from Unit
Memorandum and Articles of Association (if Public/Private Limited Company)
Partnership Deed and Certificate of Registration (if Partnership)
Name and Address of Proprietor/Certificate of Registration (if Proprietary Concern)
Registration Details from Competent Authority (if Legal Entity Other Than Above)
Copy of Certificate of Establishment of Firm/Company
Land and Property Documents
Certified Khasra (Land Record Ownership Document)
Site Map (Land Record Ownership Document)
Registered Sale Deed or Lease Deed (Title Document)
Project and Planning Documents
Copy of Project Report
Certificate from HRACC (if Applicable)
Copy of Certificate from Town and Country Planning (TNCP)
Copy of Map Sanctioned by the Local Authority (Municipal Corporation, Municipality Etc.)
Copy of the Building Permission Granted by the Appropriate Authority
Copy of Approved Floor Plan
Completion Certificate from Competent Authority
Financial Documents
Total Project Cost Certificate Certified by Certified Engineer/Valuer
Total Capital Cost Certificate Certified by CA
Debt Details from Concerned Financial Institution
Operational Documents
Proof of Date of Commercial Operation (Copy of First Lodging Bill)
Proof of Date of Commercial Operation (Copy of First Catering Bill)
Proof of Date of Commercial Operation (Copy of First Bill of Service/Facility Provided)
Photographs of Unit/Project from 3 Different Angles (JPG Format Not More Than 500 KB Size)
Banking and Identity Documents
Cancelled Cheque of the Firm/Company
Copy of PAN Card
Copy of Photo ID Proof (Aadhaar Card/ Voter ID/ Passport Etc.)
Copy of Vender Form

faqs.question: Is there a strict deadline for submitting the subsidy application form after I have commenced the commercial operations of my unit?
faqs.answer: You must submit your application within 1 year of the date of commencement of operation to be eligible for the claim.

faqs.question: Are there any mandatory employment requirements regarding local residents that I must satisfy to be eligible for the capital subsidy?
faqs.answer: Yes, to be eligible for the capital subsidy, it is mandatory to provide employment to 70% of the total staff to residents of Madhya Pradesh.

faqs.question: Once I have received the subsidy, is there a mandatory minimum period during which I must keep my tourism unit operational?
faqs.answer: You are required to run the unit for at least 3 years from the date of availing the subsidy and must submit a self-declaration regarding continuous operation annually.

faqs.question: What are the financial consequences if I am unable to sustain the operation of my unit and have to close it within three years?
faqs.answer: If the unit closes within 1, 2, or 3 years of receiving the subsidy, you must refund 80%, 60%, or 50% of the subsidy amount, respectively.

faqs.question: If I decide to establish my tourism project in a distant or remote area, are there any additional financial incentives available?
faqs.answer: Projects in such areas are eligible for an additional 5% Capital Investment Subsidy, and the minimum investment limit and room ceiling are reduced to 50%.

faqs.question: If my tourism unit qualifies for grants under more than one category, can I claim benefits from all eligible categories simultaneously?
faqs.answer: No, if a unit is entitled to a grant/subsidy in more than one category, you must choose only one category in which you wish to get the benefit.

faqs.question: I already own an operational hotel and plan to expand it; am I eligible to receive a subsidy for this expansion under the guidelines?
faqs.answer: Expansion projects are eligible for the subsidy provided there is a minimum 50% increase in the staying capacity of the existing establishment.

faqs.question: As an entrepreneur belonging to the Scheduled Caste or Scheduled Tribe category, am I entitled to any extra fiscal benefits?
faqs.answer: Yes, entrepreneurs from SC/ST categories with 100% ownership of the tourism project are entitled to an additional 5% capital investment subsidy.

faqs.question: Is the subsidy amount calculated solely based on my investment, or is there an upper limit I should be aware of?
faqs.answer: The subsidy is a percentage of your fixed capital investment, but it is strictly subject to a maximum ceiling amount specified for your project category.

faqs.question: If I have incurred capital expenditure prior to the actual commencement of the unit, will that cost be considered for the grant?
faqs.answer: Expenditure incurred up to a maximum of 05 years before commencement is considered valid if the project was executed and operated in one go.

faqs.question: To prove the capital cost incurred on my project, whose certification is required to be submitted along with the subsidy application?
faqs.answer: The total capital cost incurred must be certified by a certified Engineer/Valuer, and the total project cost must be certified by a Chartered Accountant.

faqs.question: What specific documentation must I provide to officially prove the date of commercial operation of my tourism unit?
faqs.answer: You must provide copies of the first lodging bill, catering bill, bill of service provided, or any other legally acceptable proof to prove the date of commercial operation.

faqs.question: Does my project need to be established within a specific timeframe to qualify for the capital investment benefits mentioned?
faqs.answer: Your project must be established and operationalized during the operative tenure of the policy to be entitled to the capital investment subsidy.

faqs.question: How should I submit my application for land allocation or permissions to ensure it is processed correctly by the department?
faqs.answer: All applications for land allocation, permissions, and subsidies should be submitted online through the State Government's Invest Portal or Single Window System.
